design_philosophy.core_loop: Navigate 2D sidescrolling levels by jumping and gliding, collecting marbles, selecting from multiple stages with varied paths
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Platforming precision combined with exploration of multiple stage routes and item collection
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Play as a wise-cracking cat character with personality, discover hidden paths and special items while rescuing captured relatives
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Emphasizes non-linear stage selection and multiple paths within levels (departure from predecessor's linearity), prioritizes character personality and humor as core appeal, includes optional exploration mechanics alongside core platforming

difficulty.lives_system: Bubsy can absorb three hits before losing a life (increased from predecessor's one-hit system)
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Multiple selectable stage order allows players to choose their progression path; reviewers noted the game is 'boringly easy for experienced players,' suggesting accessibility-focused design

progression.upgrade_system: Collection-based: marbles collected throughout levels; special weapons like Nerf Ballzooka gun as temporary pickups
progression.unlock_mechanism: Stage selection from a menu rather than linear progression; different paths within individual stages offer branching routes
replayability_hooks: Multiple paths through each stage encourage exploration
Multiplayer mode with second player controlling Bubsy's younger relatives
Special items and minigames scattered throughout levels

world_building_techniques: Thematic stage design representing historical eras within a virtual theme park setting
Character-driven narrative: Bubsy's personality and humor as primary narrative vehicle
Rescue mission structure: clear goal of saving niece Terri and nephew Terry
Antagonist with clear motivation: Oinker P. Hamm's time-theft scheme provides thematic coherence to level design

faction_indicators: Corrupt entrepreneur Oinker P. Hamm as antagonist representing corporate malfeasance
Bubsy as morally-aligned protagonist protecting historical integrity
danger_signals: Theme park setting with stolen historical artifacts signals exploitation of cultural heritage
Time manipulation technology used for personal profit indicates disruption of natural order
environmental_storytelling: Virtual worlds based on historical eras represent Oinker's theft scheme made visible
Theme park setting serves as thematic hub connecting disparate historical level designs
Collected marbles function as tangible evidence of exploration and progress through stolen worlds
